sequence.title: Document Backend Contract
sequence.objective: Write a backend author specification for amatrix so optional backend packages target one explicit contract.
scope: Document required backend fields and functions
Document backend selection precedence across preferred_backend, policy, and cpu
State v1 rules: dense-first acceleration, Matrix-first sparse semantics, opportunistic sparse GPU only
Include a minimal backend skeleton and a fallback example
acceptance_criteria: A docs file in the repo explains the backend contract and its required functions.
The document states that sparse is Matrix-first in v1 and GPU sparse is opportunistic.
The document defines capability ordering/meaning and backend selection precedence clearly enough for a third-party backend package to implement the contract.
The document includes at least one concrete example backend skeleton and one example of correct CPU fallback behavior.

sequence.title: Add Root Backend Integration Harness
sequence.objective: Exercise installed optional backend packages from the amatrix core package side using a shared smoke/integration harness.
scope: Run common assertions against each installed backend
Verify backend registration and core capability/status helpers
Verify dense supported routing and CPU fallback for unsupported/sparse paths
Verify precedence between preferred_backend and policy
acceptance_criteria: The core package has integration tests that exercise installed optional backend packages from amatrix, not only from the backend package repos.
The harness can skip cleanly when an optional backend package is not installed.
For each installed backend package, the harness verifies dense supported routing, CPU fallback for unsupported ops, and CPU fallback for sparse ops.
The harness reuses common assertions so new backends can be added without duplicating the whole test surface.

sequence.title: Implement Thin MLX Bridge Boundary
sequence.objective: Add the first real native/interface boundary in amatrix.mlx, starting with dense matmul, without changing the public backend contract or host-side object semantics.
scope: Add a minimal compiled or foreign-function boundary in amatrix.mlx
Keep registration and selection through the existing plugin contract
Guard unsupported systems cleanly through availability reporting
Add at least one integration test that proves the bridge path is callable through the mlx backend
acceptance_criteria: amatrix.mlx contains a minimal compiled or foreign-function bridge boundary dedicated to MLX-backed dense matmul.
The backend contract in amatrix remains unchanged and the mlx package still registers through the same plugin interface.
When MLX support is unavailable, the package reports that cleanly and falls back without changing object semantics.
At least one integration test proves the bridge path is callable behind the registered mlx backend contract, even if the implementation is initially guarded or mock-backed on non-MLX systems.

sequence.title: Implement Compact MLX QR Factor Path
sequence.objective: Replace the current explicit-Q MLX QR hot path with a true compact factor representation for pivot = FALSE so QR-heavy many-RHS workflows stop depending on resident Q materialization.
scope: Add an internal mlx_compact_qr representation under amQR
Implement a first pivot = FALSE, thin QR compact path for fast mode
Route qr.qty, qr.qy, qr.coef, qr.solve, and qr.resid through the compact representation
Keep exact mode and pivoted QR on CPU fallback until the compact MLX path is trustworthy
Benchmark the compact path against cached base QR and the current resident-Q MLX path on many-RHS workflows
acceptance_criteria: am_qr_info() can report a native compact MLX QR representation distinct from bridge_compact and resident explicit-Q paths.
many_lm(..., method = "qr") on MLX fast mode can execute its hot helper path without depending on explicit Q materialization.
The compact MLX QR path passes parity tests for pivot = FALSE helper operations and CPU fallback remains correct for exact mode.
Benchmark output shows whether the compact path improves the flagship many-RHS workflow over both cached base QR and the resident-Q MLX bridge path.
